producers.description: The 'FROM' elements of the SQL query (only the first one is in the FROM clause, others are joined in)

name.description: The name of an available producer/consumer

otherName.description: The name of the other producer to JOIN with from the list of producers

sql.description: The SQL to use to perform the join

union.description: Merges the data from the various producers in a single dataset. They must have the same output dimensions. If true, then you can't set any joins on any producer, since all producers are merged in a single dataset.

isOptional.description: If true, if the data for this producer is not available when executing the consumer (e.g. the file is not present) the consumer will not fail, but just create a placeholder dataset and continue execution.

fields.description: The fields to include in the consumer

key.description: The dimension/measure of the producer OR '*'. If '*' then return all dimensions

description.description: Optional human-readable description of this field. Descriptive metadata only; it documents intent and does not change runtime behaviour.

alias.description: If set, changes the key returned by the consumer to this alias

from.description: If the key is ambiguous, set a 'from' with the name of the producer/consumer to get the key from

transform.description: Optional list of transformations to apply to the dataset before returning the data

oneOf.description: A list of transformations to be applied in sequence

validate.description: Rules to check field value compliance and data quality. Each validation has its own rule and action to take on failure.

regex.description: Regular expression pattern to validate string fields

required.description: Whether the field value must be present

onFail.description: Action to take when validation fails

onError.description: Action to take if an error occurs during transformations

default.description: Default value of the field if it is missing (or on error if specified)

hidden.description: If set, the field is kept and used during processing, but omitted when exporting the data

fixed.description: If set, "default" must have a value. This field is not searched in the underlying dataset, but is a fixed value set by the "default" prop.

lineCount.description: If set, this field is populated with the 1-based source line number of the current record. For multi-worker file processing the final value is normalized during merge so it remains consistent across chunks.

copyFrom.description: If set, this field will be added as new to the consumer dataset and will be a copy of the specified field. Use the alias if set, otherwise the key. The source field should come before this field in the fields list.

outputs.description: Output formats and configurations for the consumer

format.description: The output format of the consumer. JSON/CSV/TXT export to a file destination; API exposes the result over the API.

exportDestination.description: The name of the source where the consumer will export its data when deployed/run

exportName.description: If the format is a file, forces the same name in the export file (extension is auto-added)

delimiter.description: Only used when the format is 'TXT'. The single-character delimiter used between fields in the exported file. Defaults to a tab if not set.

trigger.description: Triggers to perform the export (not just the usual 'Deploy')

type.description: The type of trigger schedule. CRON: time-based scheduling. API: HTTP endpoint trigger. QUEUE: SQS queue message trigger (supports shared queues with message type filtering).

value.description: The value for the trigger. For CRON: cron expression (e.g., '0 0 * * *'). For API: endpoint path. For QUEUE: SQS queue URL or queue name (will construct full URL using metadata.region and metadata.accountId if needed).

metadata.description: Additional metadata for the trigger (e.g., AWS credentials, message type filter)

messageType.description: Optional message type filter for QUEUE triggers. Only messages with matching 'type', 'messageType', or 'eventType' fields will be processed by this consumer. Messages without a matching type will be left in the queue for other consumers, enabling shared queue usage.

region.description: AWS region for the queue (for QUEUE triggers)

accountId.description: AWS account ID for constructing queue URL (for QUEUE triggers)

accessKeyId.description: AWS access key ID for queue authentication (for QUEUE triggers)

secretAccessKey.description: AWS secret access key for queue authentication (for QUEUE triggers)

sessionToken.description: AWS session token for temporary credentials (for QUEUE triggers)

method.description: Credential method for the queue (QUEUE triggers). 'arn' assumes an IAM role via STS; 'implicit'/'iam' use the AWS SDK default chain (instance/task role); omit or 'access-secret-key' to use static keys.

roleArn.description: ARN of the IAM role to assume via STS when method is 'arn' (for QUEUE triggers).

roleSessionName.description: Session name for the assumed-role credentials when method is 'arn' (for QUEUE triggers). Defaults to 'remora'.

externalId.description: Optional STS ExternalId for third-party / cross-account trust when method is 'arn' (for QUEUE triggers).

onSuccess.description: Actions to perform when the output operation completes successfully

onError.description: Actions to perform when the output operation fails

filters.description: Filters to apply to the consumer data

sql.description: The SQL to use to filter on the included dimension

member.description: The field to filter on. Use the "alias" if specified

metadata.description: Any tags to add to this consumer for sorting and organizing

project.description: Tag that groups consumers together to make them accessible by a singular api_key

distinct.description: If true, then the result set will only contain DISTINCT values

distinctOn.description: Performs a distinct operation on specific key(s) and applies collision resolution rules to determine which record to keep when duplicates are found

keys.description: The field(s) to use for determining uniqueness. Use the 'alias' if specified. Can be a single field or multiple fields for composite keys.

resolution.description: Rules to determine which record to keep when duplicates are found

strategy.description: Strategy for resolving which record to keep. 'first': Keep the first record based on orderBy field. 'last': Keep the last record based on orderBy field. 'min': Keep the record with the minimum value in orderBy field. 'max': Keep the record with the maximum value in orderBy field.

orderBy.description: Required for 'first', 'last', 'min', 'max' strategies. The field to use for ordering/comparison when selecting the record to keep.

direction.description: For 'first' and 'last' strategies, the sort direction.

pivot.description: Performs a pivot operation that transforms row values into columns. Groups data by the specified row keys, takes distinct values from the pivot column and creates a new column for each, aggregating the value column with the specified function. Cannot be used together with 'distinct' or 'distinctOn'.

rowKeys.description: The field(s) that identify a row in the pivoted output (the GROUP BY keys). Use the 'alias' if specified.

pivotColumn.description: The field whose distinct values become new columns in the output.

valueColumn.description: The field whose values are aggregated into each pivot cell.

aggregation.description: The aggregation function to apply when combining values.

pivotValues.description: If provided, only these values from the pivot column will be used as output columns. This avoids a discovery pass over the data and makes the output shape statically known. If omitted, the distinct values are discovered automatically.

columnPrefix.description: Optional prefix for the generated pivot column names (e.g. 'revenue_' produces 'revenue_East', 'revenue_West').

schema.description: The name of the JSON schema in this project that this consumer needs to adhere to

maximumFileSize.description: The maximum size of each output file, expressed in Gb. When the current output file exceeds this size it is rotated and a new file is started.

_version.description: Version number of the consumer configuration

validate.description: Dataset-level validations applied to the final result set before export

rule.description: The dataset validation rule to check

unique_fields.description: Field(s) that must have unique values across the dataset

min_rows.description: Minimum number of rows expected in the dataset

max_rows.description: Maximum number of rows allowed in the dataset

not_empty.description: Dataset must contain at least one row

onFail.description: Action to take when dataset validation fails

cast.description: Cast the value to a specific type

format.description: Optional format for date parsing or string formatting (e.g. YYYY-MM-DD, DD/MM/YY)

multiply.description: Multiply the numeric value by this factor. Use "$fieldName" to multiply by another field's value.

fields.description: Field names whose values are multiplied together.

add.description: Add this number to the numeric value. Use "$fieldName" to add another field's value.

fields.description: Field names whose values are added together.

extract.description: Extract a component from a date value

separator.description: The separator to use when joining array elements
The separator to use when splitting the string

index.description: The index of the split part to keep

pattern.description: The regex pattern to test against
The regex pattern to extract with

group.description: The capture group index to extract (0 for full match)

trim.description: Trim whitespace from both ends of the string

capitalize.description: Capitalize the first letter of the string

prepend.description: String to add at the beginning. Use "$fieldName" to prepend another field's value ("$$" escapes a literal leading $).

append.description: String to add at the end. Use "$fieldName" to append another field's value ("$$" escapes a literal leading $).

fields.description: Array of field names to combine

separator.description: Optional separator between fields (default: empty string)

template.description: Optional template string with placeholders like '{field1} - {field2}'

oneOf.description: Apply conditional logic to transform values based on comparison conditions.

clauses.description: Array of if-then clauses evaluated in order. First matching clause wins.

then.description: The value to return if the condition matches. Use "$fieldName" to return another field's value.

else.description: Default value if no clause matches. If not specified, the original value is kept. Use "$fieldName" to return another field's value.

oneOf.description: Map specific values to other values, similar to a switch/case statement. Matches are checked in order by strict equality. If no case matches, the default value is used (or the original value if no default is specified).

cases.description: Array of when/then pairs evaluated in order. First matching case wins.

when.description: The value to match against (strict equality)

then.description: The value to return if the case matches. Use "$fieldName" to return another field's value.

default.description: Default value if no case matches. If not specified, the original value is kept. Use "$fieldName" to return another field's value.

mask.description: Apply masking to the field value for data privacy. Options: 'hash' (one-way hash), 'mask' (replace with asterisks), 'crypt' (reversible encryption), 'random' (random value), 'seeded-random' (consistent random value per input), 'none' (no masking)

position.description: 0-based bit index to extract from the integer bitmask (least-significant bit = 0)

input_fmt.description: dayjs tokens describing the source date string, e.g. DDMMYYYY

output_fmt.description: dayjs tokens for the output date string, e.g. YYYY-MM-DD

strict.description: Strict parsing of the input format (default true)

oneOf.description: Map a code to a value using the output of another consumer as the lookup table. The referenced consumer is executed to build a code->value table; keep it small and cheap.

consumer.description: Name of the consumer whose output is the lookup table

key.description: Field in that consumer's output used as the map key (the code)

value.description: Field in that consumer's output returned as the mapped value

default.description: Value returned when the code is not found. If omitted, the field's onError policy applies.

comparisonCondition.description: A condition to compare a field value against. Exactly one comparison operator must be specified.

oneOf.description: Check if the numeric value is greater than the specified number

greater_than.description: The value must be greater than this number. Use "$fieldName" to compare against another field's value.

oneOf.description: Check if the numeric value is greater than or equal to the specified number

greater_than_or_equal.description: The value must be greater than or equal to this number. Use "$fieldName" to compare against another field's value.

oneOf.description: Check if the numeric value is less than the specified number

less_than.description: The value must be less than this number. Use "$fieldName" to compare against another field's value.

oneOf.description: Check if the numeric value is less than or equal to the specified number

less_than_or_equal.description: The value must be less than or equal to this number. Use "$fieldName" to compare against another field's value.

oneOf.description: Check if the value equals the specified value (strict equality)

equals.description: The value must equal this exactly. Use "$fieldName" to compare against another field's value.

oneOf.description: Check if the value does not equal the specified value

not_equals.description: The value must not equal this. Use "$fieldName" to compare against another field's value.

oneOf.description: Check if the value is in the specified list of values

in.description: The value must be one of these values. A "$fieldName" entry compares against another field's value.

oneOf.description: Check if the value is not in the specified list of values

not_in.description: The value must not be any of these values. A "$fieldName" entry compares against another field's value.

oneOf.description: Check if the string value starts with the specified prefix

starts_with.description: The string value must start with this prefix. Use "$fieldName" to compare against another field's value.

oneOf.description: Check if the string value ends with the specified suffix

ends_with.description: The string value must end with this suffix. Use "$fieldName" to compare against another field's value.

oneOf.description: Check if the string value contains the specified substring

contains.description: The string value must contain this substring. Use "$fieldName" to compare against another field's value.

oneOf.description: Check if the string value does not contain the specified substring

not_contains.description: The string value must not contain this substring. Use "$fieldName" to compare against another field's value.

oneOf.description: Check if the value is empty (null, undefined, or blank string)

is_empty.description: Returns true if the value is null, undefined, or an empty/whitespace-only string

oneOf.description: Check if the value is not empty (has content)

is_not_empty.description: Returns true if the value is not null, not undefined, and not an empty/whitespace-only string

consumerOutputOnFinish.description: Actions to perform when output operations complete

moveToDestination.description: If the action is 'move-file', this specifies the source destination in remora where the source file should be moved

consumers.description: If the action is 'run-consumer', the names of the consumers to run sequentially once this output finishes

examples.name: UserOrderAnalytics
examples.description: Consumer that combines user data with their order history
